findings.description: pluralize uses abs(count)==1, so count==-1 yields the singular ('-1 item') whereas standard English treats -1 as plural ('-1 items').
indent_block uses splitlines()+join which drops a trailing newline and normalizes CRLF/Unicode line breaks to LF, so the output is not a faithful round-trip of the input.
splitlines() also splits on Unicode line boundaries (\v, \f, \x85, \u2028, \u2029), so text containing those characters gets indented at unexpected break points.

mask_middle reveals first and last `visible` chars, so a value of length 2*visible+1 (e.g. 9 with default visible=4) leaks all but one character, weakening masking of secrets/tokens despite the docstring's claim.
slugify does not validate max_length; a negative value produces surprising slicing behavior (slug[:-n] drops trailing chars) instead of raising or clamping.

truncate counts code points via len(), so when suffix is multi-codepoint or text contains combining marks/surrogate pairs the cut can split a grapheme; acceptable if 'characters' means code points but undocumented.
truncate's default suffix is the single ellipsis character '…' (U+2026) rather than '...', which may render inconsistently in ASCII-only contexts and is undocumented as the default.

slugify silently drops all non-ASCII characters via encode('ascii','ignore') with no transliteration, so non-Latin input can yield an empty slug; behavior is documented but worth noting as a usability nit.
